meta.reportLabel: Tue - WAR-SHOCK RE-FIRES: ceasefire collapsed, Iran hit 2 UAE tankers in Omani waters (1 killed), US 3rd-night strikes, and Trump reinstated a Hormuz naval blockade + 20% cargo toll. Oil gapped ~9% (Brent ~$83, WTI ~$79); gold FELL AGAIN (-2% to ~$4,020) so the haven bid stays dead - fear priced via rates/oil, not gold. Clean vectors: long oil, long CAD, short EUR & JPY. US June CPI today 12:30 UTC (headline soft ~3.9%, core firm ~2.9%) but June oil relief is stale vs the July spike. Cleanest leans: short EUR/USD, long USOIL, long CAD/JPY.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Monday's de-escalation hope was wrong: the ceasefire effectively COLLAPSED and the war-shock re-fired, rotating the board straight back from wait-for-CPI to risk-off oil-shock. Iran struck two UAE tankers inside Omani waters (1 crew killed), the US resumed strikes for a 3rd straight night, and Trump reinstated a US naval blockade of Iranian ships + a 20% toll on ALL Hormuz cargo (CENTCOM restarts it 4 PM ET today). Oil gapped ~9% - Brent ~$83.30 (biggest one-day gain in 6yrs), WTI ~$79. The tell is still gold, which FELL AGAIN (-2% to ~$4,020, 2nd down day): the haven bid keeps failing because higher oil -> higher-for-longer -> higher real rates, so fear is priced via rates/oil, not gold or the yen. Clean vectors: long oil, long CAD, short the energy-importers (EUR, JPY), short the risk-beta highs (AUD, NZD). Overlay: US June CPI prints today 12:30 UTC but the June data is backward-looking vs the July oil spike.
meta.nextBigEvent: Tue Jul 14, 12:30 UTC US June CPI - headline seen soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9% on June's -10% gasoline) vs core firm (+0.3%, ~2.9%), but backward-looking against July's +9% oil spike so the war-shock likely dominates. Then CENTCOM's Hormuz naval blockade resumes 4 PM ET (20:00 UTC). Saturday Jul 18 Iran-Oman talks are the off-ramp that can reverse oil in minutes.

dailyRead: Monday's de-escalation hope was wrong: the ceasefire has effectively COLLAPSED and the war-shock re-fired hard - the board rotates straight back from 'wait-for-CPI' to 'risk-off oil-shock.' Iran struck two UAE tankers (al-Bahiya, Mombasa) inside Omani territorial waters - one Indian crew member killed, eight injured - the US resumed strikes for a 3rd straight night (Bandar Abbas), and, decisively, Trump reinstated a US naval blockade of Iranian ships and demanded a 20% toll on ALL cargo transiting Hormuz (CENTCOM's blockade restarts 4 PM ET today). Oil gapped ~9% - Brent settled ~$83.30 (biggest single-day gain in six years), WTI ~$79 (from ~$74) - a genuine supply-shock premium, not the capped two-way of Monday. The tell is still gold, which FELL again (-2% to ~$4,020, second down day): the haven bid keeps failing because higher oil -> higher-for-longer inflation -> higher real rates, so fear is priced through rates and oil, not gold or the yen. That leaves the clean vectors: long oil, long CAD, short the energy-importers (EUR, JPY), short the risk-beta highs (AUD, NZD). Overlaying it all, US June CPI prints today (12:30 UTC) - headline seen soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9% on June's -10% gasoline) vs core firm (+0.3% -> ~2.9%) - but the June data is backward-looking against a July oil spike the market can already see, so a soft headline is unlikely to undo the shock. Cleanest leans: short EUR/USD and long USOIL; CAD/JPY long is the pure oil-divergence trade - all sized for a CPI two-way and a Saturday Oman-talks off-ramp that can reverse oil in minutes.

today.headline: Oil bid RE-FIRES on the war-shock - WTI ~$79 (+9%), petro-CAD is the FX winner
today.read: The capped-oil read flipped: a real supply-shock (blockade + 20% toll + tanker strikes) hands CAD a genuine terms-of-trade bid, pressing USD/CAD toward/under ~1.41. USD/CAD SHORT (MED), CAD/JPY LONG (MED). Risk: a Saturday Oman deal crushes oil and the CAD bid; a risk-off flush hits CAD's risk leg.

today.headline: Hit hardest by the oil shock - ~100% energy importer, haven bid STILL dead
today.read: The yen is the biggest FX loser of an oil supply shock and the haven bounce refuses to arrive (gold fell again), so carry + the energy bill grind USD/JPY toward ~163 and the MoF zone. USD/JPY RANGE->long tilt. Risk: a blockade-panic finally snaps the haven bid on; a >163 grind forces the MoF's hand.

today.headline: Energy hit RE-INTENSIFIES - oil +9% pins the laggard at a 1-yr low (~1.14)
today.read: The oil spike is a direct terms-of-trade hit on the energy-importer euro vs a firm USD. Short EUR/USD is the cleanest FX lean (HIGH), a single soft June-headline CPI unlikely to undo it. Risk: a soft US CPI relieves it; a hawkish ECB Jul 23; an Oman deal eases the energy hit.

today.headline: Firm ~100.9 as the haven + oil-inflation bid revives on higher-for-longer
today.read: The war-shock re-fire stacks a haven + oil-inflation bid on top of the rates story. Today's CPI is the wrinkle: headline soft (~3.9%) vs core firm (~2.9%), but June oil relief is stale vs July's spike. A soft headline misread dovish dips DXY toward 100.3; a hot core + oil shock re-fire the no-cut trade.

today.headline: Gives back Monday's claw-back as the risk-off leg re-fires
today.read: The war-shock re-fire takes back the risk-beta recovery; LNG-export status only lightly offsets. MIXED->softer; AUD/CAD & AUD/JPY tilt short. Risk: an Oman deal steadies risk and lifts AUD; a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: Down-gap threat re-arms as risk-off re-fires; dovish-hike floor holds
today.read: The high-beta down-gap threat returns with the war-shock; the peaked-3.9%/higher-hurdle floor still caps the downside. NZD/CAD & NZD/JPY tilt short. NZ Q2 CPI ~Jul 16 is the swing. Risk: an Oman deal; a >4% CPI re-arms the hawks.

today.headline: Firm rate story but a risk passenger again as risk-off re-fires
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, but the war-shock makes GBP part-trade as a high-beta passenger. GBP/USD RANGE->short tilt. Risk: a hot US CPI drags cable via the dollar; a risk-off flush; UK June CPI (~Jul 22).

strength.tag: DXY ~100.9; war-shock revives the haven bid
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, SEP 1-2 hikes, zero 2026 cuts. The war-shock re-fired so the haven + oil-inflation bid returns on top of higher-for-longer; DXY firm ~100.9.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Firm into the Jul 28-29 FOMC. Today's CPI is the wrinkle: headline soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9%) vs core firm (+0.3%, 2.9%) - but June oil relief is stale vs July's +9% spike. Risk: a soft headline misread dovish dips DXY toward 100.3; a hot core + oil shock re-fire the no-cut trade. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.verdict: FIRM — but a risk passenger again
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes). As risk-off re-fires GBP part-trades as a high-beta passenger rather than purely its own rate story.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into Jul 30. Risk: a hot US CPI drags cable via the dollar; a risk-off flush hits it; UK June CPI (~Jul 22) is the domestic check. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); Jun jobs +18.2k beat (unemp 6.5% - but all part-time, mfg -17k); BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold; technical recession. The oil supply-shock re-fired the petro-currency bid - real this time, not Monday's capped move.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ The oil bid presses USD/CAD toward/under ~1.41; BoC sidelined Jul 15, internals hollow. Risk: a Saturday Oman deal crushes oil and the CAD bid; a global risk-off flush hits CAD's risk leg; a hot US CPI re-fires USD/CAD. Next BoC Jul 15.

strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% Jul 8, 6-0, but scripted dovish: headline inflation seen PEAKED at 3.9% Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move. Soft China CPI (+1%). The war-shock re-fire re-arms the high-beta down-gap threat.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ The dovish-hike floor stands but the oil-shock risk-off leg presses NZD; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: the risk-off leg hits NZD hardest; a 4%+ print re-arms the hawks; an Oman deal steadies risk. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: gives back claw-back; thin Aug tail (~36%)
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6%; headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); Aug 11 hike priced only ~36%. China CPI missed at +1%. The risk-off re-fire takes back Monday's claw-back; LNG-export status only lightly offsets.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Sticky-core floor, but the risk-off leg dominates. Risk: a re-escalation leg re-softens it; an Oman deal + a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) re-arm the hawks. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11), meeting-by-meeting. The oil +9% spike is a direct terms-of-trade hit on the energy-importer laggard, pinning it at a 1-yr low (~1.14) vs a firm USD.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Oil-inflation lifts Sept-hike bets but stays a slow forward story, not a spot bid; the firm USD leads near term. Risk: a soft US CPI relieves it; a hawkish Jul 23 core tone hands EUR a leg; an Oman deal eases the energy hit. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.verdict: WEAK — hit hardest by the oil shock
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes. The ~100% energy-importer is the biggest FX loser of the oil supply shock and the haven bid STILL isn't arriving (gold fell again) - carry + the energy bill grind USD/JPY toward ~163. MoF verbal-only, Y11.73tn precedent unused.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Carry + the energy bill grind it toward 162-163 and the MoF zone; BoJ Jul 31 hawkish. Risk: if the blockade finally sparks genuine panic, the yen haven bid snaps on; a >163 grind forces the MoF's hand. Next BoJ Jul 31.

symbols.risk: A soft headline CPI misread dovish pops it; a hawkish ECB Jul 23 core tone squeezes shorts; a Saturday Oman deal eases the energy hit.

symbols.why: Oil ~$79 (+9%) re-fires the petro-currency bid and presses USD/CAD toward/under ~1.41 - a real supply-shock, not Monday's capped move; BoC sidelined Jul 15.
symbols.risk: An Oman deal Saturday crushes oil and the CAD bid; a global risk-off flush hits CAD; a hot US CPI re-fires the long.

symbols.why: The pure oil-divergence trade: oil-bid CAD vs the ~100% energy-importer JPY whose haven bid keeps failing - both legs align with the shock.
symbols.risk: A Saturday Oman deal crushes oil and CAD; a sudden JPY haven/panic snap or a MoF strike lifts the yen.

symbols.risk: A Saturday Oman deal unwinds it fast; stacked OPEC+/Saudi/Iran spare capacity + demand fear cap the top.

symbols.risk: The blockade finally sparks a yen haven snap; a soft US CPI caps it; a MoF strike.

catalysts.note: Headline soft (-0.1% m/m -> ~3.9% on June's -10% gasoline) vs core firm (+0.3%, ~2.9%) - a split print, but backward-looking against July's +9% oil spike, so the war-shock likely dominates.
CENTCOM restarts the blockade of Iranian ships at 4 PM ET - a hard escalation step that keeps the oil premium bid.
A demanded 20% reimbursement on ALL cargo transiting the strait - a structural supply-cost shock if enforced.

catalysts.event: Strait of Hormuz - tanker strikes, ceasefire collapse
catalysts.note: Iran hit two UAE tankers in Omani waters (1 killed); US 3rd-straight-night strikes on Bandar Abbas - every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold, risk.
Hold; oil bid re-fired vs hollow internals + technical recession.
RBNZ says headline PEAKED at 3.9% - the print validates or breaks the 'higher hurdle' guidance.
Unemployment 4.5% loosening; feeds the Aug 11 RBA calculus.
Iranian officials travel to Oman on the lane/territorial-waters dispute - the live off-ramp; a deal crushes the oil/CAD premium, a breakdown re-spikes it.
Hold; core-2.5% + a fresh oil-inflation spike is the Sept-hike signal.
Warsh round 2; hold + hawkish minimalism; CPI + the oil shock frame the message.
Dissent count for GBP after services 3.7%.
Guidance with spot ~162-163 and the MoF zone hot; the oil bill hammering the yen.

geopolitics: The de-escalation bet was wrong - the ceasefire has effectively collapsed and the war-shock re-fired. Iran struck two UAE tankers (al-Bahiya and Mombasa) with missiles inside Omani territorial waters in the southern Strait - an Indian crew member was killed and eight more (six Indians, two Ukrainians) injured - while claiming it had disabled two 'rogue supertankers.' The US resumed strikes for a third consecutive night (three explosions in Bandar Abbas), and Trump notified Congress that 'limited' military action had resumed after the ceasefire collapsed. Decisively, Trump reinstated a US naval blockade of Iranian ships and demanded a 20% toll on ALL cargo transiting Hormuz; CENTCOM says the blockade restarts at 4 PM ET Tuesday. The one off-ramp: Iranian officials travel to Oman on Saturday for talks on the lane/territorial-waters dispute, and indirect US-Iran contacts continue via Qatar - so a reversal is one headline away, but for now escalation, not diplomacy, is driving. FX transmission (Tuesday, shock re-fired): oil gapped ~9% - Brent settled ~$83.30 (biggest one-day gain in six years), WTI ~$79 (from ~$74) - a genuine supply-shock premium as ~20% of world oil-and-gas trade transits a blockaded, tolled strait. CAD is the FX winner - the petro-currency bid presses USD/CAD toward/under ~1.41. The USD is firm (DXY ~100.9) with the haven + oil-inflation bid stacked on higher-for-longer. Gold is the tell: it fell again (-2% to ~$4,020, second down day) DESPITE the escalation - the haven bid keeps failing because higher oil feeds higher-for-longer inflation and higher real rates, so fear is priced through rates and oil, not gold. EUR and JPY, the two big energy-importers, are the FX losers - EUR pinned at a 1-yr low, and the yen hit hardest (~100% import bill) with the haven bounce still refusing to arrive, so carry grinds USD/JPY toward ~163 and the MoF zone. AUD/NZD give back Monday's claw-back as the risk-off leg re-fires (AUD's LNG status a light offset); GER40 faces the oil + risk-off double headwind again; silver is squeezed by a failing haven leg and a growth-fear industrial leg. Overlaying it all, US June CPI prints at 12:30 UTC - but the June oil relief is stale against the July spike, so a soft headline is unlikely to undo the shock, and a hot core would compound it. The board is oil-shock-led with a CPI overlay and a Saturday-Oman off-ramp - which is why conviction sits in the oil-divergence trades (short EUR/USD, long USOIL, long CAD/JPY, short AUD/CAD & NZD/CAD), sized for a two-way print and a headline-driven reversal.
